title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs migration, release gates, rollback, recovery, operations, and defense from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for production publication, migration, and recovery dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 6041 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.

Inspect the Production Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 1566 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.
